meta.primaryTopic: Using GitHub Copilot effectively in daily workflows with structured prompts

draft.title: How to Use GitHub Copilot Prompts That Actually Work

frontMatter.title: How to Use GitHub Copilot Prompts That Actually Work
frontMatter.excerpt: A practical guide to writing better prompts for GitHub Copilot with real workflow examples.
frontMatter.coverAlt: Developer writing structured prompts on a laptop

draft.markdownBody:
## Intro
Most people do not get poor AI output because the model is weak. They get poor output because the prompt is vague.

## Problem
As mentioned in the transcript, results improved only when context, constraints, and expected format were combined in a single instruction.

## Solution
Start with role, then goal, then constraints, then output shape. Keep prompts short and iterate.

## Examples
As mentioned in the transcript, one large prompt produced generic advice, while smaller iteration rounds produced practical and testable steps.

## Conclusion
Use a repeatable prompt structure, validate outputs quickly, and keep a short feedback loop.

sentenceVarietyWarnings: Two adjacent sentences start with imperative verbs in the solution section.
